title: NCEA Level 3 Mechanics – Practice Paper 1
instructions: Answer ALL questions. Show all working for short and long answer questions. Calculators are permitted. g = 9.81 m/s².

questions.questionText: A car starts from rest and accelerates uniformly at 2 m/s² for 8 seconds. What is its final velocity?
questions.explanation: Using v = u + at with u = 0, a = 2 m/s², t = 8 s gives v = 16 m/s.

questions.questionText: A ball is launched at 45° to the horizontal at 20 m/s. Which component of velocity is zero at the highest point of its trajectory?
questions.modelAnswer: The vertical velocity is zero at the peak of the trajectory.
questions.explanation: At maximum height, the ball momentarily stops moving vertically (vy = 0) before starting to fall. Horizontal velocity is constant throughout.

questions.questionText:
A 500 g ball is attached to a 1.2 m string and swung in a horizontal circle. The ball completes 3 full revolutions per second.
(a) Calculate the angular velocity of the ball.
(b) Calculate the centripetal acceleration.
(c) Calculate the tension in the string.
questions.explanation: This is a standard circular motion problem using ω = 2πf, a = ω²r, and F = ma.

questions.questionText:
A 2 kg trolley moving at 5 m/s to the right collides with a stationary 3 kg trolley. After the collision, the 2 kg trolley moves at 1 m/s to the right.
(a) Calculate the velocity of the 3 kg trolley after the collision.
(b) Calculate the kinetic energy before and after the collision.
(c) Is this collision elastic or inelastic? Justify your answer.
questions.modelAnswer:
(a) Conservation of momentum: 2×5 + 3×0 = 2×1 + 3×v. 10 = 2 + 3v. v = 8/3 ≈ 2.67 m/s to the right.
(b) KE_before = ½×2×25 = 25 J. KE_after = ½×2×1 + ½×3×(8/3)² = 1 + 10.67 ≈ 11.67 J.
(c) Inelastic – kinetic energy decreased from 25 J to ≈ 11.67 J. Energy was lost to heat/sound/deformation.
questions.explanation: Use conservation of momentum to find the unknown velocity, then compare KE before and after.
